stories.headline: Iran war live: Tehran insists on control of Hormuz amid reports of US talks
stories.summary: Washington and Tehran have agreed to stop attacks and renew talks in the Qatari capital, Doha, Axios reports.

stories.headline: US says it has agreed deal with Iran to halt strikes and resume talks

stories.headline: US, Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
stories.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US, Iran To Halt Attacks; Korea Looks to Cement AI Lead | The Asia Trade 6/29/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Paul Allen, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US and Iran agree to pause attacks and meet in Qatar
stories.summary: US and Iran agree to halt strikes and hold talks in Qatar on Tuesday over Strait of Hormuz tensions,  US media reports.

stories.headline: Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan, Taliban officials say
stories.summary: Pakistan says its strikes were a response to "terrorist attacks". The Taliban labelled them "cowardly".

stories.headline: Pakistan strikes eastern Afghanistan killing dozens following Karachi attack

stories.headline: Pakistan Strikes Kill 29 Militants in New Afghan Border Flare-Up
stories.summary: Pakistan said it carried out a ground operation and air strikes along its border with Afghanistan on Sunday and Monday, killing 29 militants in response to earlier attacks and marking the biggest flare-up in cross-border violence in weeks.

stories.headline: Verizon and BT Create Joint Venture for International Businesses
stories.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. and the UK’s BT Group Plc have agreed to create a joint venture for their international businesses that will have a combined yearly revenue of about $4 billion.

stories.summary: Eternal Ltd. and Swiggy Ltd. got the 10-minute delivery party going in India. Now, e-commerce titans Amazon.com Inc. and Walmart Inc.’s Flipkart are looking to crash it.

stories.headline: FTSE 100 Futures Steady as Oil Stays Around $72
PBOC’s New Overnight Rate Said Below Forecasts in Hint at Easing
stories.summary: China’s central bank set the interest rate on its new overnight liquidity tool at a level that was below expectations, according to people familiar with the matter, in what some economists see as a de facto rate cut that could push down market borrowing costs.

stories.summary: Pakistan is seeking to buy liquefied natural gas for delivery this week as a string of attacks in the Strait of Hormuz disrupts flows of the super-chilled fuel.

stories.headline: Alebund Pharmaceuticals on Business Outlook

stories.summary: Autonomous-driving firm Momenta Global Ltd. started taking investor orders for its Hong Kong initial public offering to raise HK$5.9 billion ($752 million), pouncing on a multiyear high for share sales in the city.

stories.headline: Markets Are Buying Into Less Uncertainty: Capital.com
stories.summary: Kyle Rodda of Capital.com shares his views on the de-dollarization trade and market volatility. He says it shouldn't be understated "how significant it is that the United States is inviting Iran, effectively, back into global trade, selling its oil into global markets." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump reveals new images of passport with his portrait to mark America’s 250th
stories.summary: The State Department said the new document “boldly honors” the president and the nation’s founding. The rendering differed from previous images of the passport in several ways.

stories.headline: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
stories.summary: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.
